name: ReAct Framework
desc: Interleave reasoning traces and actions in LLM agents
desc_zh: 在大模型代理中交织推理轨迹与动作执行
steps: Define the agent's task scope and available tool set (APIs, search, code exec)
Instrument the LLM prompt to emit Thought / Action / Observation traces
Execute each Action against real tools and feed Observation back into context
Repeat the Thought-Action-Observation loop until the stopping condition is met
Evaluate trace quality and prune or summarize long reasoning chains for efficiency
steps_zh: 定义代理任务范围及可用工具集（API、搜索、代码执行等）
在提示词中要求模型输出 Thought / Action / Observation 三段式轨迹
将每个 Action 发送至真实工具并将 Observation 回写入上下文
循环执行推理-行动-观察，直至达到终止条件
评估轨迹质量，裁剪或摘要过长推理链以降低成本
origin_source: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models (ICLR 2023, arXiv 2022)
origin_source_zh: ReAct：在语言模型中协同推理与行动（ICLR 2023，arXiv 2022）
when_to_use: Building LLM agents that need to interact with external tools or APIs
Tasks requiring multi-step reasoning with real-world information retrieval
Scenarios where the model must show its reasoning chain for auditability
Complex question-answering that cannot be solved with a single LLM call
when_to_use_zh: 构建需要与外部工具或 API 交互的大模型代理
需要多步推理并结合真实信息检索的任务
要求模型展示推理链以满足审计需求的场景
单次大模型调用无法解决的复杂问答任务
core_concepts: Thought-Action-Observation loop: the agent alternates between reasoning, acting, and observing results
Grounded reasoning: each reasoning step is validated against real tool outputs rather than hallucinated
Trace-based debugging: the full reasoning chain is logged, making failures diagnosable
Dynamic stopping: the agent decides when it has enough information to produce a final answer
core_concepts_zh: Thought-Action-Observation 循环：代理在推理、行动和观察结果之间交替执行
接地推理：每个推理步骤通过真实工具输出验证，而非凭空生成
基于轨迹的调试：完整推理链被记录，使故障可诊断
动态停止：代理自行判断何时已获取足够信息以生成最终答案
timeline: Shunyu Yao et al. publish the ReAct paper on arXiv
ReAct accepted at ICLR 2023; gains wide adoption in agent frameworks
LangChain integrates ReAct as a default agent executor pattern
ReAct variants emerge: ReWOO, Reflexion, and Plan-and-Solve extend the paradigm
ReAct becomes the foundational pattern in most production AI agent systems
timeline_zh: Shunyu Yao 等人在 arXiv 发布 ReAct 论文
ReAct 被 ICLR 2023 接收，在代理框架中广泛采用
LangChain 将 ReAct 集成为默认代理执行器模式
ReAct 变体涌现：ReWOO、Reflexion、Plan-and-Solve 扩展了该范式
ReAct 成为大多数生产级 AI 代理系统的基础模式
dos: Provide clear, concise tool descriptions so the model selects the right action
Limit the maximum number of loop iterations to prevent runaway costs
Log the full Thought-Action-Observation trace for debugging and evaluation
Use few-shot examples in the prompt to guide the reasoning format
dos_zh: 提供清晰简洁的工具描述，确保模型选择正确的动作
限制最大循环迭代次数以防止成本失控
记录完整的 Thought-Action-Observation 轨迹用于调试和评估
在提示词中使用少样本示例引导推理格式
donts: Don't give the agent access to irreversible or dangerous tools without safeguards
Don't skip observation validation - always verify tool outputs before feeding back
Don't allow unbounded context growth; summarize or truncate long traces
Don't rely solely on ReAct for tasks that require long-horizon planning
donts_zh: 不要在没有安全保障的情况下让代理访问不可逆或危险的工具
不要跳过观察验证——始终在回传前核实工具输出
不要允许上下文无限增长，对过长轨迹进行摘要或截断
不要仅依赖 ReAct 处理需要长期规划的任务
case_study: LangChain adopted the ReAct pattern as the core execution loop for its Agent framework, enabling thousands of developers to build tool-augmented LLM applications. By standardizing the Thought-Action-Observation cycle, LangChain reduced the complexity of building agents from scratch and made reasoning traces inspectable for debugging.
case_study_zh: LangChain 将 ReAct 模式作为其 Agent 框架的核心执行循环，使数千名开发者能够构建工具增强的大模型应用。通过标准化「思考-行动-观察」循环，LangChain 降低了从零构建代理的复杂性，并使推理轨迹可供调试检查。
when_not_to_use: Simple single-turn tasks that don't require tool interaction
Latency-critical applications where multi-step loops add unacceptable delay
Tasks with very long planning horizons that exceed context window limits
Scenarios where deterministic rule-based logic is more reliable than LLM reasoning
when_not_to_use_zh: 不需要工具交互的简单单轮任务
对延迟极度敏感、多步循环会带来不可接受延迟的应用
规划范围极长、超出上下文窗口限制的任务
确定性规则逻辑比大模型推理更可靠的场景

name: RAG Architecture
desc: Ground LLM responses with retrieved external knowledge
desc_zh: 通过检索外部知识为大模型响应提供事实依据
steps: Chunk, embed, and index domain documents into a vector store
At query time, embed the user question and retrieve top-K relevant chunks
Construct a prompt that injects retrieved context before the question
Generate the answer with the LLM, citing source chunks for traceability
Evaluate retrieval recall and generation faithfulness; iterate on chunking strategy
steps_zh: 对领域文档进行分块、向量化并写入向量数据库
查询时将用户问题向量化，检索 Top-K 相关片段
构建提示词，将检索内容注入问题前作为上下文
由大模型生成答案并引用来源片段以确保可溯源
评估检索召回率与生成忠实度，迭代优化分块策略
origin_author: Patrick Lewis et al. (Meta AI / University College London)
origin_source: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks (NeurIPS 2020)
origin_source_zh: 面向知识密集型 NLP 任务的检索增强生成（NeurIPS 2020）
when_to_use: Enterprise knowledge bases where accuracy and source citation are required
Domains with rapidly changing information that cannot be baked into model weights
Customer-facing Q&A systems that must minimize hallucination
Compliance-sensitive applications requiring traceable, auditable answers
when_to_use_zh: 需要准确性和来源引用的企业知识库
信息快速变化、无法固化到模型权重中的领域
必须最小化幻觉的面向客户的问答系统
要求可追溯、可审计答案的合规敏感型应用
core_concepts: Retrieval-then-generate: fetch relevant context from an external store before prompting the LLM
Chunking strategy: document splitting approach directly impacts retrieval quality and relevance
Embedding similarity: vector distance measures how semantically close a chunk is to the query
Faithfulness evaluation: measuring whether the generated answer is grounded in retrieved evidence
core_concepts_zh: 先检索后生成：在提示大模型之前从外部存储获取相关上下文
分块策略：文档切分方式直接影响检索质量和相关性
嵌入相似度：向量距离衡量片段与查询的语义接近程度
忠实度评估：衡量生成的答案是否基于检索到的证据
timeline: Patrick Lewis et al. publish the RAG paper, introducing retrieval-augmented generation
ChatGPT launch sparks massive interest in grounding LLMs with external knowledge
Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma) see explosive growth driven by RAG adoption
Advanced RAG techniques emerge: hybrid search, reranking, GraphRAG, and agentic RAG
RAG becomes standard architecture for enterprise AI assistants worldwide
timeline_zh: Patrick Lewis 等人发表 RAG 论文，提出检索增强生成方法
ChatGPT 发布引发大规模兴趣，推动用外部知识为大模型提供依据
向量数据库（Pinecone、Weaviate、Chroma）因 RAG 采用而爆发式增长
高级 RAG 技术涌现：混合检索、重排序、GraphRAG 和代理式 RAG
RAG 成为全球企业 AI 助手的标准架构
dos: Experiment with multiple chunking strategies (fixed-size, semantic, document-structure-aware)
Use hybrid retrieval combining dense embeddings with sparse keyword search (BM25)
Add a reranking step to improve precision of retrieved chunks before generation
Include source citations in the generated output for user trust and verification
dos_zh: 尝试多种分块策略（固定大小、语义分块、文档结构感知）
使用混合检索，将稠密嵌入与稀疏关键词搜索（BM25）结合
在生成前添加重排序步骤以提高检索片段的精确度
在生成输出中包含来源引用，增强用户信任和可验证性
donts: Don't assume embeddings alone capture all query nuances - augment with keyword filters
Don't use excessively large chunks that dilute relevant information with noise
Don't skip evaluation of retrieval recall - poor retrieval means poor generation
Don't ignore chunk overlap; missing context at boundaries causes fragmented answers
donts_zh: 不要假设嵌入能捕获所有查询细微差别——用关键词过滤补充
不要使用过大的分块，那会用噪声稀释相关信息
不要跳过检索召回率评估——检索差意味着生成差
不要忽略分块重叠问题，边界处缺少上下文会导致答案碎片化
case_study: Stripe built an internal RAG-powered documentation assistant that indexes their vast developer docs and internal knowledge base. By retrieving relevant API specifications and support articles before generation, the system reduced hallucinated answers by over 80% compared to a vanilla LLM and cut average developer support resolution time significantly.
case_study_zh: Stripe 构建了基于 RAG 的内部文档助手，对其庞大的开发者文档和内部知识库进行索引。通过在生成前检索相关 API 规范和支持文章，系统将幻觉答案减少了 80% 以上，并显著缩短了开发者支持的平均解决时间。
when_not_to_use: Creative or open-ended generation tasks where grounding is unnecessary
Small, static datasets that can be fully included in the system prompt
Real-time applications where retrieval latency is prohibitive
Tasks where the LLM's parametric knowledge alone is sufficient and up to date
when_not_to_use_zh: 不需要事实依据的创意或开放式生成任务
可以完整放入系统提示词的小型静态数据集
检索延迟不可接受的实时应用
大模型参数知识已足够且时效性满足需求的任务

name: Multi-Agent Orchestration Pattern
desc: Coordinate specialized AI agents via an orchestrator layer
desc_zh: 通过编排层协调多个专职 AI 代理协同工作
steps: Decompose the overall task into sub-tasks with clear input/output contracts
Assign each sub-task to a specialized agent with the appropriate model and tools
Implement an orchestrator agent that routes tasks and aggregates results
Define inter-agent communication schemas (structured JSON messages or function calls)
Add circuit-breakers and fallback paths to handle agent failures gracefully
steps_zh: 将整体任务分解为具有明确输入输出契约的子任务
为每个子任务指定配备合适模型与工具的专职代理
实现负责任务路由和结果聚合的编排代理
定义代理间通信模式（结构化 JSON 消息或函数调用）
添加熔断器与降级路径以优雅处理代理故障
origin_author: Qingyun Wu et al. (Microsoft Research) / CrewAI / LangGraph
origin_source: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation (2023); CrewAI and LangGraph frameworks
origin_source_zh: AutoGen：通过多代理对话实现下一代大模型应用（2023）；CrewAI 和 LangGraph 框架
when_to_use: Complex workflows requiring diverse expertise that no single agent can cover
Tasks with naturally parallelizable sub-components (research + code + review)
Systems that benefit from separation of concerns between planning, execution, and verification
Scenarios requiring role-based collaboration such as debate, review, or consensus
when_to_use_zh: 需要多元专业能力、单个代理无法覆盖的复杂工作流
具有天然可并行子组件的任务（研究 + 编码 + 审查）
规划、执行和验证之间关注点分离有益的系统
需要基于角色协作（辩论、审查或共识）的场景
core_concepts: Orchestrator pattern: a central agent decomposes tasks, delegates to specialists, and synthesizes results
Agent specialization: each agent has a focused role, model configuration, and tool set
Inter-agent messaging: structured communication protocols enable reliable handoffs between agents
Failure isolation: circuit-breakers prevent one failing agent from cascading failures to the system
core_concepts_zh: 编排器模式：中央代理分解任务、委派给专家并综合结果
代理专业化：每个代理具有专注的角色、模型配置和工具集
代理间消息传递：结构化通信协议确保代理间可靠交接
故障隔离：熔断器防止单个代理故障级联到整个系统
timeline: Microsoft Research publishes AutoGen, pioneering multi-agent conversation frameworks
CrewAI launches, introducing role-based multi-agent orchestration for developers
LangGraph provides graph-based agent orchestration with stateful workflows
OpenAI Swarm and Anthropic multi-agent patterns validate the orchestration paradigm
Multi-agent orchestration becomes standard for complex enterprise AI workflows
timeline_zh: 微软研究院发布 AutoGen，开创多代理对话框架
CrewAI 推出，引入面向开发者的基于角色的多代理编排
LangGraph 提供基于图的代理编排和有状态工作流
OpenAI Swarm 和 Anthropic 多代理模式验证了编排范式
多代理编排成为复杂企业 AI 工作流的标准方案
dos: Define clear role boundaries and responsibilities for each agent
Use structured message schemas for inter-agent communication
Implement timeout and retry policies for each agent independently
Start with 2-3 agents and scale complexity only when proven necessary
dos_zh: 为每个代理定义清晰的角色边界和职责
使用结构化消息模式进行代理间通信
为每个代理独立实现超时和重试策略
从 2-3 个代理开始，仅在证明必要时才增加复杂度
donts: Don't create too many agents - complexity grows non-linearly with agent count
Don't allow agents to communicate in unstructured free text without validation
Don't skip the single-agent baseline - prove multi-agent adds value over simpler approaches
Don't let agents share mutable state without synchronization mechanisms
donts_zh: 不要创建过多代理——复杂性随代理数量非线性增长
不要允许代理以未验证的自由文本进行通信
不要跳过单代理基线——需证明多代理比简单方案更有价值
不要让代理在没有同步机制的情况下共享可变状态
case_study: Microsoft developed AutoGen to power multi-agent workflows within their enterprise products. In internal testing, a three-agent system (planner, coder, reviewer) achieved 25% higher task completion rates on complex software engineering benchmarks compared to single-agent approaches. The orchestration pattern has since been adopted across Microsoft Copilot products.
case_study_zh: 微软开发了 AutoGen 来驱动其企业产品中的多代理工作流。在内部测试中，三代理系统（规划者、编码者、审查者）在复杂软件工程基准测试上的任务完成率比单代理方案高出 25%。该编排模式已在微软 Copilot 系列产品中广泛采用。
when_not_to_use: Simple tasks that a single well-prompted agent can handle effectively
Latency-sensitive applications where multi-agent coordination adds unacceptable overhead
Projects with limited budget where multiple LLM calls multiply cost significantly
Early prototyping stages where the task decomposition is not yet well understood
when_not_to_use_zh: 单个经过良好提示的代理即可有效处理的简单任务
多代理协调带来不可接受开销的延迟敏感型应用
预算有限、多次大模型调用会显著增加成本的项目
任务分解尚不明确的早期原型阶段

name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
desc: Insert human checkpoints into automated AI workflows
desc_zh: 在自动化 AI 工作流中插入人工审核节点
steps: Map the workflow and identify high-risk or high-uncertainty decision points
Define escalation criteria that trigger human review (confidence threshold, risk score)
Design low-friction review UIs that surface AI reasoning alongside the decision
Capture human feedback as labeled training signal for continuous model improvement
Instrument approval latency and human override rates to tune automation thresholds
steps_zh: 梳理工作流，识别高风险或高不确定性的决策节点
定义触发人工审核的升级条件（置信度阈值、风险评分）
设计低摩擦的审核界面，将 AI 推理过程与决策并排呈现
将人工反馈作为标注训练信号用于持续模型优化
监控审批延迟与人工覆盖率，动态调整自动化阈值
origin_author: Concept from active learning research; popularized by Saleema Amershi et al. (Microsoft Research)
origin_source: Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction (CHI 2019); Power to the People: The Role of Humans in Interactive Machine Learning (AI Magazine, 2014)
origin_source_zh: 人机交互设计指南（CHI 2019）；赋能于人：人类在交互式机器学习中的角色（AI Magazine，2014）
when_to_use: High-stakes decisions where AI errors have significant real-world consequences
Regulatory-compliant workflows that mandate human approval (finance, healthcare, legal)
Early-stage AI deployments where model reliability is not yet fully established
Continuously improving systems that use human corrections as training signal
when_to_use_zh: AI 错误会产生重大现实后果的高风险决策
法规要求人工审批的合规工作流（金融、医疗、法律）
模型可靠性尚未完全建立的早期 AI 部署
利用人工纠正作为训练信号的持续改进系统
core_concepts: Escalation thresholds: confidence scores or risk levels that trigger human review
Feedback loop: human corrections feed back into model retraining and threshold calibration
Asymmetric automation: automate low-risk decisions fully while keeping humans for high-risk ones
Review UI design: presenting AI reasoning and evidence to help humans make fast, informed decisions
core_concepts_zh: 升级阈值：触发人工审核的置信度分数或风险等级
反馈回路：人工纠正回馈到模型重训练和阈值校准
非对称自动化：低风险决策完全自动化，高风险决策保留人工
审核界面设计：展示 AI 推理和证据，帮助人类快速做出知情决策
timeline: AI Magazine publishes influential work on humans in interactive machine learning
Microsoft Research publishes Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction at CHI 2019
ChatGPT launch highlights the critical need for human oversight in AI systems
EU AI Act formalizes human oversight requirements for high-risk AI systems
Major cloud providers integrate human-in-the-loop workflows into AI platforms
timeline_zh: AI Magazine 发表关于人类在交互式机器学习中角色的重要研究
微软研究院在 CHI 2019 发布「人机交互设计指南」
ChatGPT 发布凸显了 AI 系统中人工监督的关键需求
欧盟 AI 法案正式规定高风险 AI 系统的人工监督要求
主要云服务商将人机协同工作流集成到 AI 平台中
dos: Set clear escalation criteria based on measurable confidence scores or risk levels
Design review interfaces that show AI reasoning and supporting evidence together
Track human override rates and use them to calibrate automation thresholds over time
Use human feedback systematically as labeled data for model improvement
dos_zh: 基于可衡量的置信度分数或风险等级设定明确的升级标准
设计展示 AI 推理过程和支持证据的审核界面
跟踪人工覆盖率并用于逐步校准自动化阈值
系统性地将人工反馈用作模型改进的标注数据
donts: Don't make human review a bottleneck by sending too many low-value escalations
Don't ignore human feedback - if overrides are frequent, the model needs improvement
Don't design review UIs that force humans to start from scratch instead of reviewing AI work
Don't assume human reviewers are infallible - track reviewer accuracy and consistency too
donts_zh: 不要因发送过多低价值升级请求而让人工审核成为瓶颈
不要忽视人工反馈——如果覆盖频繁，说明模型需要改进
不要设计迫使人工从头开始而非审查 AI 工作的审核界面
不要假设人工审核者不会出错——也需跟踪审核者的准确性和一致性
case_study: Scale AI built its entire business model around human-in-the-loop workflows for AI training data. Their platform routes AI-generated annotations through calibrated human review stages, where reviewers correct and validate labels. This approach enabled their clients, including OpenAI and Meta, to achieve significantly higher data quality than fully automated labeling.
case_study_zh: Scale AI 围绕 AI 训练数据的人机协同工作流构建了整个商业模式。其平台将 AI 生成的标注通过校准的人工审核阶段路由，审核者纠正和验证标签。这种方法使其客户（包括 OpenAI 和 Meta）获得了远高于全自动标注的数据质量。
when_not_to_use: Fully automated systems where latency from human review is unacceptable
Low-risk decisions where the cost of occasional AI errors is negligible
High-volume tasks where human review cannot scale to match throughput
Mature models with proven reliability that no longer benefit from human oversight
when_not_to_use_zh: 人工审核延迟不可接受的全自动化系统
偶尔 AI 出错的成本可忽略不计的低风险决策
人工审核无法扩展到匹配吞吐量的高容量任务
已证明可靠性、不再需要人工监督的成熟模型

name: Prompt Chaining Pattern
desc: Decompose complex tasks into sequential prompt stages
desc_zh: 将复杂任务分解为顺序执行的提示词阶段
steps: Break the end-to-end task into discrete, independently testable prompt stages
Define the output schema of each stage as the typed input of the next
Validate and sanitize intermediate outputs before passing them downstream
Add a gating step that checks quality criteria before continuing the chain
Log each stage's input/output pair for debugging and prompt regression testing
steps_zh: 将端到端任务拆分为独立可测试的离散提示词阶段
将每个阶段的输出模式定义为下一阶段的类型化输入
在传递给下游前对中间输出进行验证和清洗
添加质量门控步骤，满足标准后再继续执行链路
记录每个阶段的输入输出对，用于调试与提示词回归测试
origin_author: Emerged from prompt engineering practice; formalized by Harrison Chase (LangChain) and Anthropic's prompt engineering guides
origin_source: LangChain Sequential Chains (2022); Anthropic Prompt Engineering Guide (2023)
origin_source_zh: LangChain 顺序链（2022）；Anthropic 提示工程指南（2023）
when_to_use: Complex tasks that benefit from divide-and-conquer decomposition
Workflows where each stage needs different instructions or system prompts
Pipelines requiring intermediate validation or quality gating between steps
Scenarios where debugging requires inspecting each step independently
when_to_use_zh: 受益于分治法分解的复杂任务
每个阶段需要不同指令或系统提示词的工作流
步骤之间需要中间验证或质量门控的管道
调试时需要独立检查每个步骤的场景
core_concepts: Stage decomposition: breaking a complex task into sequential, focused prompt stages
Schema contracts: each stage defines typed input/output schemas for reliable handoff
Quality gates: validation checkpoints between stages that prevent error propagation
Regression testing: logging stage I/O pairs enables prompt version regression detection
core_concepts_zh: 阶段分解：将复杂任务拆分为顺序执行的专注提示词阶段
模式契约：每个阶段定义类型化输入输出模式以确保可靠交接
质量门控：阶段间的验证检查点防止错误传播
回归测试：记录阶段输入输出对以检测提示词版本回归
timeline: LangChain introduces SequentialChain as a core abstraction for multi-step LLM workflows
Anthropic publishes prompt engineering guides formalizing chaining best practices
OpenAI Cookbook documents prompt chaining patterns for production applications
Structured output support from major LLMs makes schema-based chaining more reliable
Prompt chaining becomes a foundational pattern taught in AI engineering curricula
timeline_zh: LangChain 引入 SequentialChain 作为多步大模型工作流的核心抽象
Anthropic 发布提示工程指南，正式化链式调用最佳实践
OpenAI Cookbook 记录面向生产应用的提示词链模式
主流大模型支持结构化输出，使基于模式的链式调用更可靠
提示词链成为 AI 工程课程中教授的基础模式
dos: Define clear typed schemas for inputs and outputs at each chain stage
Add validation and quality gates between stages to catch errors early
Log all intermediate inputs and outputs for debugging and regression testing
Keep each stage focused on a single well-defined sub-task
dos_zh: 为每个链路阶段定义清晰的类型化输入输出模式
在阶段之间添加验证和质量门控以尽早捕获错误
记录所有中间输入输出用于调试和回归测试
让每个阶段专注于单个明确定义的子任务
donts: Don't chain too many stages - each stage adds latency and potential failure points
Don't pass unvalidated output from one stage to the next without checking
Don't use the same system prompt for every stage - tailor it to each stage's task
Don't ignore error propagation; a failure in stage N can corrupt all downstream stages
donts_zh: 不要链接过多阶段——每个阶段都会增加延迟和潜在故障点
不要在未验证的情况下将一个阶段的输出传给下一个阶段
不要对每个阶段使用相同的系统提示词——应针对各阶段任务定制
不要忽视错误传播，第 N 阶段的失败可能破坏所有下游阶段
case_study: Anthropic uses prompt chaining extensively in Claude's internal content processing pipeline. Complex user requests are decomposed into stages: intent classification, context gathering, response generation, and safety verification. Each stage has independent evaluation metrics, enabling targeted improvements without disrupting the full chain.
case_study_zh: Anthropic 在 Claude 的内部内容处理管道中广泛使用提示词链。复杂用户请求被分解为多个阶段：意图分类、上下文收集、响应生成和安全验证。每个阶段有独立的评估指标，支持针对性改进而不影响整条链路。
when_not_to_use: Simple tasks that a single prompt can handle well without decomposition
Real-time applications where multi-stage latency is unacceptable
Exploratory or creative tasks where rigid stage boundaries constrain the output
Scenarios where the task decomposition is unclear or highly dynamic
when_not_to_use_zh: 单个提示词即可良好处理、无需分解的简单任务
多阶段延迟不可接受的实时应用
刚性阶段边界会限制输出的探索性或创意任务
任务分解不明确或高度动态的场景

name: AI Pair Programming Model
desc: Structure developer and AI collaboration in the coding loop
desc_zh: 在编码循环中结构化人类开发者与 AI 的协作方式
steps: Establish role division: human owns intent and review; AI owns synthesis and drafting
Use intent-first prompting - describe what, why, and constraints before asking for code
Review AI output for correctness, security, and style before accepting each chunk
Feed failing tests or error messages back to the AI as grounding for refinement
Reflect at session end: categorize AI contributions and identify skill gaps to address
steps_zh: 明确角色分工：人类负责意图与审查，AI 负责合成与起草
使用意图优先提示——在请求代码前先描述目标、原因和约束
在接受每段代码前审查 AI 输出的正确性、安全性和风格
将失败测试或错误信息反馈给 AI 作为细化的依据
在会话结束时复盘：归类 AI 贡献并识别需补足的技能差距
origin_author: GitHub (Oege de Moor, Albert Ziegler et al.)
origin_source: GitHub Copilot Technical Preview (2021); Productivity Assessment of Neural Code Completion (2022)
origin_source_zh: GitHub Copilot 技术预览版（2021）；神经代码补全的生产力评估（2022）
when_to_use: Day-to-day software development where AI can accelerate boilerplate and drafting
Learning new programming languages or frameworks with AI as a knowledgeable tutor
Refactoring sessions where AI can suggest patterns and handle repetitive transformations
Debugging sessions where AI can analyze error messages and propose fixes
when_to_use_zh: AI 可加速样板代码和起草的日常软件开发
以 AI 作为知识渊博导师学习新编程语言或框架
AI 可建议设计模式并处理重复性转换的重构会话
AI 可分析错误信息并提出修复建议的调试会话
core_concepts: Role asymmetry: human provides intent, context, and judgment; AI provides speed and synthesis
Intent-first prompting: describing goals and constraints before requesting code yields better results
Iterative refinement: treating AI output as a first draft to be reviewed, tested, and refined
Skill amplification: AI handles mechanical tasks while human focuses on architecture and design
core_concepts_zh: 角色不对称：人类提供意图、上下文和判断；AI 提供速度和合成能力
意图优先提示：在请求代码前描述目标和约束能获得更好的结果
迭代细化：将 AI 输出视为需要审查、测试和细化的初稿
技能放大：AI 处理机械性任务，人类专注于架构和设计
timeline: GitHub launches Copilot Technical Preview, pioneering AI pair programming at scale
GitHub Copilot becomes generally available; studies show 55% faster task completion
GPT-4 enables chat-based pair programming with deeper reasoning capabilities
Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf emerge as AI-native coding environments
AI pair programming becomes standard practice adopted by over 70% of professional developers
timeline_zh: GitHub 推出 Copilot 技术预览版，率先大规模实现 AI 结对编程
GitHub Copilot 正式发布；研究显示任务完成速度提升 55%
GPT-4 实现基于对话的结对编程，具备更深层推理能力
Claude Code、Cursor 和 Windsurf 作为 AI 原生编码环境出现
AI 结对编程成为超过 70% 专业开发者采用的标准实践
dos: Always review AI-generated code for correctness, security vulnerabilities, and style
Provide rich context (project structure, conventions, test expectations) to the AI
Use failing tests and error messages as concrete feedback for AI refinement
Maintain your own understanding of the code - don't accept what you can't explain
dos_zh: 始终审查 AI 生成代码的正确性、安全漏洞和风格
向 AI 提供丰富上下文（项目结构、约定、测试期望）
使用失败测试和错误信息作为 AI 细化的具体反馈
保持对代码的自主理解——不要接受无法解释的代码
donts: Don't blindly accept AI-generated code without understanding it
Don't skip security review - AI may generate code with known vulnerability patterns
Don't use AI as a crutch that prevents you from learning the underlying concepts
Don't share proprietary or sensitive code with AI tools without checking data policies
donts_zh: 不要在不理解的情况下盲目接受 AI 生成的代码
不要跳过安全审查——AI 可能生成含已知漏洞模式的代码
不要将 AI 当作阻碍自己学习底层概念的拐杖
不要在未确认数据政策的情况下与 AI 工具共享专有或敏感代码
case_study: GitHub's internal study of Copilot showed that developers using AI pair programming completed tasks 55% faster than those without it. More importantly, developers reported higher satisfaction and spent more time on creative problem-solving rather than boilerplate, demonstrating that the model works best when humans focus on design intent while AI handles synthesis.
case_study_zh: GitHub 对 Copilot 的内部研究表明，使用 AI 结对编程的开发者完成任务的速度比未使用者快 55%。更重要的是，开发者报告了更高的满意度，将更多时间用于创造性问题解决而非样板代码，证明了当人类专注于设计意图、AI 处理合成时该模型效果最佳。
when_not_to_use: Security-critical code that requires formal verification rather than AI-assisted drafting
Proprietary algorithms that cannot be exposed to third-party AI services
Situations where developers need to deeply learn a concept rather than get quick answers
Regulated codebases with strict provenance requirements for every line of code
when_not_to_use_zh: 需要形式化验证而非 AI 辅助起草的安全关键代码
不能暴露给第三方 AI 服务的专有算法
开发者需要深入学习概念而非获取快速答案的场景
对每行代码有严格来源追溯要求的受监管代码库

name: Guardrails Framework
desc: Enforce input/output constraints on LLM-powered systems
desc_zh: 对大模型驱动系统强制执行输入输出约束
steps: Enumerate risk categories relevant to the application (PII, toxicity, hallucination, injection)
Implement input validators that reject or rewrite unsafe or malformed prompts
Add output validators that check factual grounding, format compliance, and policy rules
Route policy violations to fallback responses or human escalation queues
Collect violation logs and retrain or tune guardrail classifiers on production data
steps_zh: 列举应用相关的风险类别（个人信息、有害内容、幻觉、注入攻击）
实现输入验证器，拒绝或改写不安全或格式错误的提示词
添加输出验证器，检查事实依据、格式合规性和策略规则
将策略违规路由至降级响应或人工升级队列
收集违规日志并在生产数据上重新训练或调整护栏分类器
origin_author: Shreya Rajpal (Guardrails AI); NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails team
origin_source: Guardrails AI open-source library (2023); NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails (2023)
origin_source_zh: Guardrails AI 开源库（2023）；NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails（2023）
when_to_use: Production LLM applications that handle user-facing content and must avoid harmful outputs
Systems processing sensitive data where PII leakage or injection attacks are risks
Applications requiring structured, schema-compliant outputs from LLMs
Regulated industries (finance, healthcare) where AI output must meet compliance standards
when_to_use_zh: 处理面向用户内容、必须避免有害输出的生产级大模型应用
处理敏感数据、存在个人信息泄露或注入攻击风险的系统
要求大模型输出结构化、符合模式的应用
AI 输出必须满足合规标准的受监管行业（金融、医疗）
core_concepts: Input validation: filtering, rewriting, or rejecting unsafe prompts before they reach the LLM
Output validation: checking generated content against policy rules, format schemas, and factual grounding
Layered defense: combining multiple guardrail techniques (regex, classifiers, LLM-based judges) for robustness
Continuous learning: using production violation logs to improve guardrail classifiers over time
core_concepts_zh: 输入验证：在提示词到达大模型前进行过滤、改写或拒绝不安全内容
输出验证：根据策略规则、格式模式和事实依据检查生成内容
分层防御：组合多种护栏技术（正则、分类器、基于大模型的评判器）以增强鲁棒性
持续学习：利用生产违规日志持续改进护栏分类器
timeline: Guardrails AI releases the open-source guardrails library for LLM output validation
NVIDIA releases NeMo Guardrails for programmable LLM safety rails
OpenAI introduces moderation endpoint and system-level safety layers
Guardrails become standard middleware in enterprise LLM deployment stacks
Industry converges on layered guardrail architectures combining classifiers and LLM judges
timeline_zh: Guardrails AI 发布用于大模型输出验证的开源护栏库
NVIDIA 发布 NeMo Guardrails 可编程大模型安全护栏
OpenAI 推出内容审核端点和系统级安全层
护栏成为企业大模型部署技术栈中的标准中间件
行业趋向于融合分类器和大模型评判器的分层护栏架构
dos: Implement guardrails on both input and output - defense in depth is essential
Use multiple validation techniques (regex, classifiers, LLM judges) in layers for robustness
Log all guardrail triggers with full context for analysis and classifier improvement
Test guardrails with adversarial red-teaming to find bypasses before attackers do
dos_zh: 在输入和输出两端都实现护栏——纵深防御至关重要
分层使用多种验证技术（正则、分类器、大模型评判器）以增强鲁棒性
记录所有护栏触发事件及完整上下文，用于分析和分类器改进
通过对抗性红队测试发现绕过漏洞，抢在攻击者之前
donts: Don't rely solely on prompt-based guardrails - they can be bypassed by prompt injection
Don't make guardrails so strict that they block legitimate user requests excessively
Don't treat guardrails as set-and-forget - they need continuous tuning based on production data
Don't ignore false positives; excessive blocking erodes user trust and adoption
donts_zh: 不要仅依赖基于提示词的护栏——它们可能被提示注入绕过
不要让护栏过于严格以致过度阻止合法用户请求
不要将护栏视为一劳永逸——需要基于生产数据持续调优
不要忽视误报，过度拦截会侵蚀用户信任和采用率
case_study: NVIDIA developed NeMo Guardrails to protect enterprise LLM deployments in production. The framework provides programmable rails that intercept both inputs and outputs, enforcing topic boundaries and safety policies. Enterprise adopters reported reducing harmful output incidents by over 90% while maintaining natural conversational quality.
case_study_zh: NVIDIA 开发了 NeMo Guardrails 来保护企业生产环境中的大模型部署。该框架提供可编程护栏，拦截输入和输出，强制执行主题边界和安全策略。企业采用者报告有害输出事件减少了 90% 以上，同时保持了自然的对话质量。
when_not_to_use: Internal-only research tools where output safety is not a concern
Creative writing applications where rigid constraints would limit expression
Prototype or hackathon projects where speed of iteration outweighs safety requirements
Systems where all inputs are pre-validated and outputs are only consumed by code
when_not_to_use_zh: 输出安全性不是关注点的纯内部研究工具
严格约束会限制表达的创意写作应用
迭代速度优先于安全要求的原型或黑客松项目
所有输入已预验证且输出仅被代码消费的系统

name: Context Window Management Pattern
desc: Strategically manage LLM context to maximize coherence
desc_zh: 战略性管理大模型上下文以最大化对话连贯性
steps: Profile token usage across system prompt, history, retrieved context, and output budget
Implement a sliding-window or summary-compression strategy for long conversation history
Assign priority tiers to context segments (system > recent turns > retrieved > background)
Prune low-priority segments first when approaching the token limit
Benchmark coherence and task performance across window sizes to calibrate the strategy
steps_zh: 分析系统提示词、历史记录、检索上下文和输出预算各自的 Token 占用
对长对话历史实现滑动窗口或摘要压缩策略
为上下文片段分配优先级层级（系统 > 近期轮次 > 检索内容 > 背景知识）
接近 Token 上限时优先裁剪低优先级片段
在不同窗口大小下基准测试连贯性和任务性能以校准策略
origin_author: Emerged from practical LLM engineering; formalized by OpenAI and Anthropic engineering teams
origin_source: OpenAI API Best Practices (2023); Anthropic Long Context Guide (2024); MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems (2023)
origin_source_zh: OpenAI API 最佳实践（2023）；Anthropic 长上下文指南（2024）；MemGPT：迈向操作系统化的大模型（2023）
when_to_use: Long-running conversations that risk exceeding the model's context window
RAG systems where retrieved content competes with conversation history for token budget
Multi-turn assistants that need to maintain coherence across many exchanges
Cost-sensitive applications where reducing context size directly lowers API costs
when_to_use_zh: 有超出模型上下文窗口风险的长时间对话
检索内容与对话历史争夺 Token 预算的 RAG 系统
需要在多轮交互中保持连贯性的多轮助手
减少上下文大小可直接降低 API 成本的成本敏感型应用
core_concepts: Token budgeting: allocating fixed token budgets to system prompt, history, context, and output
Priority-based pruning: removing lowest-priority context segments first when nearing limits
Summary compression: replacing older conversation turns with concise summaries to free tokens
Sliding window: keeping only the N most recent turns while archiving or summarizing older ones
core_concepts_zh: Token 预算分配：为系统提示词、历史记录、上下文和输出分配固定的 Token 预算
基于优先级的裁剪：接近上限时优先移除最低优先级的上下文片段
摘要压缩：用简洁摘要替代较旧的对话轮次以释放 Token
滑动窗口：仅保留最近 N 轮对话，对更旧的内容进行归档或摘要
timeline: GPT-4 launches with 8K/32K context windows, making context management critical
MemGPT paper proposes virtual context management inspired by OS memory hierarchies
Anthropic releases Claude with 200K context window, shifting management strategies
Google Gemini offers 1M+ token context, but management remains important for cost and coherence
Context management evolves into a standard discipline in LLM application architecture
timeline_zh: GPT-4 以 8K/32K 上下文窗口发布，使上下文管理变得至关重要
MemGPT 论文提出受操作系统内存层次启发的虚拟上下文管理
Anthropic 发布 200K 上下文窗口的 Claude，改变了管理策略
Google Gemini 提供 100 万+ Token 上下文，但管理对成本和连贯性仍然重要
上下文管理演变为大模型应用架构中的标准学科
dos: Profile and monitor token usage across all context segments in production
Implement summary compression for conversation history exceeding a threshold
Reserve sufficient token budget for the model's output generation
Test coherence and task accuracy at different context utilization levels
dos_zh: 在生产环境中分析和监控所有上下文片段的 Token 使用情况
对超过阈值的对话历史实现摘要压缩
为模型的输出生成预留足够的 Token 预算
在不同上下文利用率水平下测试连贯性和任务准确性
donts: Don't naively truncate context from the beginning - you may lose critical system instructions
Don't fill the entire context window; leave room for the model to reason and generate
Don't assume larger context windows eliminate the need for management - cost and coherence degrade
Don't mix high-priority and low-priority information without clear segmentation
donts_zh: 不要简单地从头部截断上下文——可能丢失关键系统指令
不要填满整个上下文窗口，要为模型的推理和生成留出空间
不要假设更大的上下文窗口消除了管理需求——成本和连贯性会下降
不要在没有明确分段的情况下混合高优先级和低优先级信息
case_study: OpenAI's ChatGPT manages context windows across millions of concurrent conversations. The system uses a combination of sliding windows and conversation summaries to maintain coherence within token limits. When context exceeds the budget, older turns are progressively summarized, preserving key facts and user preferences while freeing tokens for the current exchange.
case_study_zh: OpenAI 的 ChatGPT 在数百万并发对话中管理上下文窗口。系统结合使用滑动窗口和对话摘要在 Token 限制内保持连贯性。当上下文超出预算时，较旧的轮次被逐步摘要，保留关键事实和用户偏好，同时为当前交互释放 Token。
when_not_to_use: Short single-turn interactions that never approach context limits
Batch processing tasks where each request is independent and stateless
Applications using models with context windows far exceeding typical input sizes
Offline analysis tasks where cost and latency are not primary concerns
when_not_to_use_zh: 永远不会接近上下文限制的短单轮交互
每个请求独立且无状态的批处理任务
使用上下文窗口远超典型输入大小的模型的应用
成本和延迟不是主要关注点的离线分析任务

name: Tool-Use Design Pattern
desc: Design agent-callable tools with reliable interfaces
desc_zh: 为 AI 代理设计可靠可调用的工具接口
steps: Define each tool as a typed function with a machine-readable JSON Schema description
Write tool descriptions from the model's perspective - emphasize when to use and not use
Implement idempotent tool handlers with deterministic error messages the model can act on
Add a tool-selection evaluation harness to measure call accuracy on representative queries
Version and deprecate tools explicitly, updating descriptions to guide migration
steps_zh: 将每个工具定义为带有机器可读 JSON Schema 描述的类型化函数
从模型视角撰写工具描述——强调何时使用以及何时不应使用
实现幂等的工具处理器，返回模型可据此行动的确定性错误信息
构建工具选择评估框架，在代表性查询上度量调用准确率
显式对工具进行版本管理和废弃标注，更新描述以引导迁移
origin_author: OpenAI Function Calling team; Anthropic Tool Use team; Timo Schick et al.
origin_source: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools (Meta AI, 2023); OpenAI Function Calling (2023)
origin_source_zh: Toolformer：语言模型可以自学使用工具（Meta AI，2023）；OpenAI 函数调用（2023）
when_to_use: Building LLM agents that need to interact with external APIs and services
Applications requiring deterministic operations (math, database queries, file I/O) within LLM workflows
Systems where the LLM must select the right tool from a large catalog based on user intent
Production agents that need reliable, versioned, and well-documented tool interfaces
when_to_use_zh: 构建需要与外部 API 和服务交互的大模型代理
在大模型工作流中需要确定性操作（数学、数据库查询、文件 I/O）的应用
大模型需要根据用户意图从大型工具目录中选择正确工具的系统
需要可靠、版本化且文档完善的工具接口的生产代理
core_concepts: JSON Schema tool definitions: machine-readable descriptions that tell the model what each tool does and expects
Model-perspective descriptions: writing tool docs optimized for LLM comprehension rather than human developers
Idempotent handlers: ensuring tools can be safely retried without side effects on failure
Tool selection evaluation: measuring how accurately the model picks the right tool for a given query
core_concepts_zh: JSON Schema 工具定义：告知模型每个工具的功能和期望的机器可读描述
模型视角描述：为大模型理解而非人类开发者优化的工具文档
幂等处理器：确保工具在失败时可安全重试而无副作用
工具选择评估：衡量模型针对给定查询选择正确工具的准确性
timeline: Meta AI publishes Toolformer, showing LLMs can learn to use tools autonomously
OpenAI launches Function Calling in the Chat Completions API
Anthropic introduces Tool Use for Claude, enabling structured tool interactions
Tool-use becomes a standard feature across all major LLM providers
MCP (Model Context Protocol) and open standards emerge for universal tool interoperability
timeline_zh: Meta AI 发布 Toolformer，展示大模型可自主学习使用工具
OpenAI 在 Chat Completions API 中推出函数调用功能
Anthropic 为 Claude 引入工具使用功能，实现结构化工具交互
工具使用成为所有主要大模型提供商的标准功能
MCP（模型上下文协议）和开放标准涌现，实现通用工具互操作性
dos: Write tool descriptions from the model's perspective - be explicit about when to use and when not to
Use JSON Schema to define typed parameters with clear descriptions and constraints
Make tool handlers idempotent so retries are safe and error messages are actionable
Evaluate tool selection accuracy regularly with representative query benchmarks
dos_zh: 从模型视角编写工具描述——明确说明何时使用和何时不应使用
使用 JSON Schema 定义带有清晰描述和约束的类型化参数
使工具处理器幂等，确保重试安全且错误消息可操作
定期用代表性查询基准评估工具选择准确性
donts: Don't write vague tool descriptions - ambiguity causes the model to misselect tools
Don't expose irreversible side effects without confirmation mechanisms
Don't provide too many similar tools - consolidate to reduce selection confusion
Don't return unstructured error messages; the model needs parseable feedback to recover
donts_zh: 不要编写模糊的工具描述——歧义会导致模型错误选择工具
不要在没有确认机制的情况下暴露不可逆的副作用
不要提供太多相似工具——合并以减少选择混淆
不要返回非结构化错误信息，模型需要可解析的反馈来恢复
case_study: Anthropic designed Claude's tool-use system with strict JSON Schema definitions and model-perspective documentation. By requiring typed parameters and deterministic error responses, Claude achieves high tool selection accuracy even with large tool catalogs. The approach has become a reference implementation for the industry's best practices in agent tool design.
case_study_zh: Anthropic 使用严格的 JSON Schema 定义和模型视角文档设计了 Claude 的工具使用系统。通过要求类型化参数和确定性错误响应，Claude 即使在大型工具目录中也能实现高工具选择准确率。该方案已成为行业代理工具设计最佳实践的参考实现。
when_not_to_use: Purely conversational applications that don't need external tool access
Tasks where all required knowledge is within the model's parametric memory
Prototypes where hardcoding a single tool call is simpler than building a tool framework
Scenarios where deterministic code logic is more reliable than LLM-driven tool selection
when_not_to_use_zh: 不需要外部工具访问的纯对话应用
所需知识全在模型参数记忆中的任务
硬编码单个工具调用比构建工具框架更简单的原型
确定性代码逻辑比大模型驱动的工具选择更可靠的场景

name: AI-First API Design
name_zh: AI 优先 API 设计
desc: Design APIs optimized for consumption by AI agents
desc_zh: 面向 AI 代理消费优化设计 API 接口
steps: Expose semantic resource names and self-describing endpoints (OpenAPI + rich descriptions)
Return structured, schema-validated JSON responses that avoid ambiguous free-text fields
Provide bulk and idempotent operations to reduce agent round-trips and retries
Embed task-relevant metadata (next actions, related resources) in responses as hints
Publish a machine-readable capability manifest so agents can discover and compose APIs
steps_zh: 暴露语义化资源名称和自描述端点（OpenAPI + 丰富描述）
返回模式验证的结构化 JSON，避免歧义自由文本字段
提供批量操作和幂等操作以减少代理往返请求和重试
在响应中嵌入任务相关元数据（后续动作、关联资源）作为提示
发布机器可读的能力清单，使代理能够发现和组合 API
origin_author: Emerging practice from Anthropic MCP team, OpenAI Plugins team, and API-first companies
origin_source: Anthropic Model Context Protocol (MCP, 2024); OpenAI Plugins and Actions (2023); API design community best practices
origin_source_zh: Anthropic 模型上下文协议（MCP，2024）；OpenAI 插件与动作（2023）；API 设计社区最佳实践
when_to_use: Building APIs that will be primarily consumed by AI agents rather than human developers
Creating tool ecosystems for LLM platforms (ChatGPT plugins, Claude MCP servers)
Enterprise systems where AI agents need to discover and compose APIs autonomously
Modernizing existing APIs to be compatible with AI agent consumption patterns
when_to_use_zh: 构建主要由 AI 代理而非人类开发者消费的 API
为大模型平台创建工具生态系统（ChatGPT 插件、Claude MCP 服务器）
AI 代理需要自主发现和组合 API 的企业系统
改造现有 API 以兼容 AI 代理的消费模式
core_concepts: Self-describing endpoints: APIs that include rich semantic descriptions machines can understand
Structured responses: schema-validated JSON that eliminates ambiguity for agent consumption
Capability manifests: machine-readable declarations of what an API can do, enabling autonomous discovery
Hypermedia hints: embedding next-action suggestions and related resources in responses to guide agents
core_concepts_zh: 自描述端点：包含机器可理解的丰富语义描述的 API
结构化响应：消除代理消费歧义的模式验证 JSON
能力清单：API 功能的机器可读声明，实现自主发现
超媒体提示：在响应中嵌入下一步动作建议和关联资源以引导代理
timeline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Plugins, establishing AI-consumable API patterns
OpenAI evolves Plugins into GPT Actions with improved API integration
Anthropic releases Model Context Protocol (MCP) as an open standard for AI-tool interop
AI-first API design becomes a recognized discipline as agent ecosystems mature
MCP adoption accelerates with support from major IDE and cloud providers
timeline_zh: OpenAI 推出 ChatGPT 插件，确立 AI 可消费的 API 模式
OpenAI 将插件演进为 GPT Actions，改进 API 集成
Anthropic 发布模型上下文协议（MCP）作为 AI 工具互操作的开放标准
随着代理生态系统成熟，AI 优先 API 设计成为公认学科
MCP 获得主要 IDE 和云服务商支持，采用加速
dos: Provide rich OpenAPI descriptions with semantic resource names and usage examples
Return structured, schema-validated responses with consistent error formats
Include hypermedia links and next-action hints to guide agent navigation
Design idempotent operations so agents can safely retry on failure
dos_zh: 提供带有语义资源名称和使用示例的丰富 OpenAPI 描述
返回具有一致错误格式的结构化、模式验证响应
包含超媒体链接和下一步动作提示以引导代理导航
设计幂等操作使代理在失败时可安全重试
donts: Don't use ambiguous free-text response fields that require interpretation
Don't require complex multi-step authentication flows that agents struggle with
Don't return HTML or unstructured formats when the consumer is an AI agent
Don't break backward compatibility without versioning and deprecation notices
donts_zh: 不要使用需要解读的模糊自由文本响应字段
不要要求代理难以处理的复杂多步认证流程
当消费者是 AI 代理时不要返回 HTML 或非结构化格式
不要在没有版本控制和弃用通知的情况下破坏向后兼容性
case_study: Anthropic created the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to standardize how AI agents interact with external tools and data sources. MCP provides a universal protocol for AI-first API design, enabling any tool to be exposed to Claude and other LLMs through a consistent interface. The open standard has been adopted across the industry, powering thousands of MCP servers.
case_study_zh: Anthropic 创建了模型上下文协议（MCP）来标准化 AI 代理与外部工具和数据源的交互方式。MCP 为 AI 优先 API 设计提供了通用协议，使任何工具都能通过一致接口暴露给 Claude 和其他大模型。该开放标准已在行业中广泛采用，驱动数千个 MCP 服务器。
when_not_to_use: APIs primarily consumed by human developers through traditional client applications
Internal microservice-to-microservice communication where agents are not involved
Legacy systems where redesigning APIs is not feasible or cost-effective
Simple CRUD APIs where standard REST conventions are sufficient
when_not_to_use_zh: 主要由人类开发者通过传统客户端应用消费的 API
不涉及代理的内部微服务间通信
重新设计 API 不可行或不划算的遗留系统
标准 REST 约定已足够的简单 CRUD API

name: Self-Healing Systems Pattern
desc: Use AI agents to detect, diagnose, and remediate failures
desc_zh: 利用 AI 代理自动检测、诊断并修复系统故障
steps: Instrument the system with rich structured logs, metrics, and health-check endpoints
Deploy a monitoring agent that correlates signals and classifies failure modes
Maintain a runbook knowledge base the agent uses to select remediation actions
Execute low-risk remediations automatically; escalate high-risk ones for human approval
Record every incident-action-outcome triple to continuously improve the runbook
steps_zh: 为系统配备丰富的结构化日志、指标和健康检查端点
部署监控代理，关联信号并分类故障模式
维护运维手册知识库供代理选择修复动作
自动执行低风险修复操作；将高风险操作升级至人工审批
记录每次故障-动作-结果三元组，持续完善运维手册
origin_author: IBM Autonomic Computing initiative (2001); modernized by AIOps pioneers (Moogsoft, BigPanda, Shoreline.io)
origin_source: The Vision of Autonomic Computing (IBM, 2003); AIOps: Real-World Challenges and Research Innovations (2023)
origin_source_zh: 自主计算愿景（IBM，2003）；AIOps：现实挑战与研究创新（2023）
when_to_use: Large-scale distributed systems where manual incident response is too slow
Cloud-native environments with frequent, predictable failure patterns
24/7 operations where immediate automated response prevents cascading outages
Systems with well-documented runbooks that can be translated into automated actions
when_to_use_zh: 手动事故响应太慢的大规模分布式系统
具有频繁、可预测故障模式的云原生环境
需要即时自动响应以防止级联中断的全天候运营
具有可转化为自动动作的完善运维手册的系统
core_concepts: Observe-Orient-Decide-Act: the AI agent continuously monitors, diagnoses, decides, and remediates
Runbook automation: codifying human operational knowledge into machine-executable remediation playbooks
Risk-tiered remediation: auto-executing safe fixes while escalating risky ones for human approval
Feedback learning: recording incident-action-outcome triples to improve future diagnosis and remediation
core_concepts_zh: 观察-定向-决策-行动：AI 代理持续监控、诊断、决策和修复
运维手册自动化：将人类运维知识编码为机器可执行的修复剧本
分级修复：自动执行安全修复，将高风险操作升级至人工审批
反馈学习：记录故障-动作-结果三元组以改进未来的诊断和修复
timeline: IBM launches the Autonomic Computing initiative, envisioning self-managing systems
AIOps emerges as a category, applying ML to IT operations monitoring
LLM-powered AIOps agents begin replacing rule-based runbook automation
Shoreline.io and PagerDuty integrate LLM agents for intelligent incident remediation
Self-healing patterns become standard in cloud-native infrastructure management
timeline_zh: IBM 启动自主计算计划，设想自我管理的系统
AIOps 作为一个类别出现，将机器学习应用于 IT 运维监控
基于大模型的 AIOps 代理开始取代基于规则的运维手册自动化
Shoreline.io 和 PagerDuty 集成大模型代理进行智能事故修复
自愈模式成为云原生基础设施管理的标准方案
dos: Start with well-understood, low-risk remediations and gradually expand the automation scope
Maintain comprehensive runbook documentation that the AI agent can reference
Log every remediation action with full context for post-incident review and learning
Implement circuit-breakers to prevent the healing agent from making things worse
dos_zh: 从理解充分、低风险的修复操作开始，逐步扩大自动化范围
维护 AI 代理可参考的完整运维手册文档
记录每个修复动作及完整上下文用于事后审查和学习
实现熔断器以防止自愈代理使情况恶化
donts: Don't automate high-risk remediations without human approval gates
Don't deploy self-healing agents without extensive testing in staging environments
Don't let the agent operate without guardrails - define clear boundaries of what it can do
Don't assume the agent's diagnosis is always correct; maintain human escalation paths
donts_zh: 不要在没有人工审批门控的情况下自动执行高风险修复
不要在未经预发布环境充分测试的情况下部署自愈代理
不要让代理在没有护栏的情况下运行——明确定义其操作边界
不要假设代理的诊断总是正确的，保持人工升级路径
case_study: Netflix pioneered self-healing infrastructure practices at scale. Their systems automatically detect and remediate common failure patterns such as unhealthy instances, capacity bottlenecks, and configuration drift. By combining chaos engineering with AI-driven remediation, Netflix achieves 99.99% availability while reducing mean time to recovery from hours to minutes.
case_study_zh: Netflix 率先在大规模环境中实践自愈基础设施。其系统自动检测并修复常见故障模式，如不健康实例、容量瓶颈和配置漂移。通过将混沌工程与 AI 驱动的修复结合，Netflix 在将平均恢复时间从数小时缩短到数分钟的同时实现了 99.99% 的可用性。
when_not_to_use: Small systems where manual incident response is fast and sufficient
Environments with unpredictable, novel failure modes that lack runbook coverage
Systems where automated remediation carries regulatory or safety risks
Early-stage products where operational patterns are not yet well understood
when_not_to_use_zh: 手动事故响应足够快且充分的小型系统
缺乏运维手册覆盖的不可预测新型故障模式的环境
自动修复带有监管或安全风险的系统
运维模式尚未充分了解的早期产品

name: AI Observability Framework
name_zh: AI 系统可观测性框架
desc: Trace, monitor, and explain LLM system behavior in prod
desc_zh: 在生产环境中追踪、监控并解释大模型系统行为
steps: Instrument every LLM call with trace IDs linking prompt, model version, and raw output
Collect latency, token cost, and quality metrics per call and aggregate into dashboards
Implement an LLM-as-judge evaluator that scores output quality on sampled traffic
Set up drift alerts when quality or distribution metrics deviate from baseline
Store prompt-response pairs in an evaluation dataset for offline regression testing
steps_zh: 为每次大模型调用打上关联提示词、模型版本和原始输出的追踪 ID
收集每次调用的延迟、Token 成本和质量指标并汇总至仪表盘
实现 LLM-as-Judge 评估器，对采样流量的输出质量打分
当质量或分布指标偏离基线时触发漂移告警
将提示词-响应对存入评估数据集，用于离线回归测试
origin_author: LLM observability community; pioneered by Arize AI, LangSmith (LangChain), and Weights & Biases
origin_source: Arize AI LLM Observability Platform (2023); LangSmith Tracing and Evaluation (2023); OpenTelemetry for LLMs proposals
origin_source_zh: Arize AI 大模型可观测性平台（2023）；LangSmith 追踪与评估（2023）；面向大模型的 OpenTelemetry 提案
when_to_use: Production LLM applications where output quality must be continuously monitored
Systems requiring cost tracking and optimization of LLM API spending
Applications where model upgrades or prompt changes need regression detection
Compliance-sensitive deployments that require audit trails for AI decisions
when_to_use_zh: 需要持续监控输出质量的生产级大模型应用
需要追踪和优化大模型 API 开支的系统
模型升级或提示词变更需要回归检测的应用
要求 AI 决策审计跟踪的合规敏感型部署
core_concepts: LLM tracing: linking every call to its prompt, model version, parameters, and output for full reproducibility
Quality metrics: automated scoring of output quality using LLM-as-judge or custom evaluators
Drift detection: alerting when output quality, latency, or cost distributions shift from established baselines
Evaluation datasets: curated prompt-response pairs used for offline regression testing across model or prompt versions
core_concepts_zh: 大模型追踪：将每次调用与其提示词、模型版本、参数和输出关联以实现完全可复现
质量指标：使用 LLM-as-Judge 或自定义评估器自动对输出质量打分
漂移检测：当输出质量、延迟或成本分布偏离基线时触发告警
评估数据集：用于跨模型或提示词版本离线回归测试的精选提示词-响应对
timeline: Arize AI launches dedicated LLM observability features for production monitoring
LangSmith releases as an integrated tracing and evaluation platform for LangChain
Weights & Biases adds LLM-specific tracing and evaluation to their MLOps platform
OpenTelemetry community begins standardizing LLM observability instrumentation
AI observability becomes a required capability in enterprise LLM deployment checklists
timeline_zh: Arize AI 推出专用大模型可观测性功能用于生产监控
LangSmith 作为 LangChain 的集成追踪和评估平台发布
Weights & Biases 在其 MLOps 平台中添加大模型特定的追踪和评估
OpenTelemetry 社区开始标准化大模型可观测性插桩
AI 可观测性成为企业大模型部署检查清单中的必备能力
dos: Instrument every LLM call with trace IDs that link prompts, outputs, and metadata
Set up automated quality evaluation on sampled production traffic using LLM judges
Create baseline metrics and alert on significant deviations in quality, latency, or cost
Build evaluation datasets from production data for offline regression testing
dos_zh: 为每次大模型调用打上关联提示词、输出和元数据的追踪 ID
使用大模型评判器对采样生产流量设置自动化质量评估
创建基线指标并在质量、延迟或成本显著偏离时告警
从生产数据构建评估数据集用于离线回归测试
donts: Don't log sensitive user data in traces without proper anonymization and access controls
Don't rely solely on manual spot-checking - automate quality evaluation at scale
Don't ignore cost metrics; unmonitored token usage can lead to surprise bills
Don't skip baselining before deploying model or prompt changes to production
donts_zh: 不要在没有适当匿名化和访问控制的情况下在追踪中记录敏感用户数据
不要仅依赖人工抽查——在规模化场景下自动化质量评估
不要忽视成本指标，未监控的 Token 使用可能导致意外账单
不要在将模型或提示词变更部署到生产环境之前跳过基线建立
case_study: Arize AI built a comprehensive LLM observability platform that traces every call, evaluates output quality, and detects drift in production. One enterprise customer reduced their hallucination rate by 40% after implementing Arize's monitoring, as the drift alerts enabled them to catch prompt degradation within hours of deployment rather than days.
case_study_zh: Arize AI 构建了综合性大模型可观测性平台，追踪每次调用、评估输出质量并检测生产中的漂移。一家企业客户在实施 Arize 监控后将幻觉率降低了 40%，因为漂移告警使他们能在部署后数小时内而非数天后发现提示词退化。
when_not_to_use: Prototype or experimental LLM applications where monitoring overhead is premature
Offline batch processing where real-time observability is unnecessary
Personal or hobby projects where the cost of observability tooling is unjustified
One-off data analysis tasks where reproducibility is not a concern
when_not_to_use_zh: 监控开销为时过早的原型或实验性大模型应用
不需要实时可观测性的离线批处理
可观测性工具成本不合理的个人或业余项目
可复现性不是关注点的一次性数据分析任务

name: Responsible AI Design Framework
name_zh: 负责任 AI 设计框架
desc: Embed fairness, safety, and accountability in AI systems
desc_zh: 在 AI 系统中内嵌公平性、安全性与问责机制
steps: Conduct an AI impact assessment covering bias, privacy, autonomy, and misuse vectors
Define measurable fairness metrics and test across demographic and use-case slices
Implement transparency features: model cards, decision explanations, confidence scores
Establish an accountability chain mapping each AI decision to a responsible human owner
Run red-team adversarial exercises and publish a responsible-use policy for the system
steps_zh: 开展 AI 影响评估，覆盖偏见、隐私、自主性和滥用向量
定义可量化的公平性指标，在人群和用例切片上进行测试
实现透明度特性：模型卡、决策解释、置信度分数
建立问责链，将每项 AI 决策映射到负责任的人类负责人
开展红队对抗演练，并发布系统的负责任使用政策
origin_author: Microsoft Responsible AI team; Google PAIR; Timnit Gebru, Margaret Mitchell et al.
origin_source: Model Cards for Model Reporting (FAT* 2019); Microsoft Responsible AI Standard (2022); EU AI Act (2024)
origin_source_zh: 模型报告的模型卡（FAT* 2019）；微软负责任 AI 标准（2022）；欧盟 AI 法案（2024）
when_to_use: Any AI system that makes decisions affecting people's lives, opportunities, or rights
Regulated industries where AI compliance with fairness and transparency laws is required
Public-facing AI products where trust and reputation depend on responsible behavior
Organizations establishing AI governance frameworks and ethical review processes
when_to_use_zh: 任何影响人们生活、机会或权利的 AI 决策系统
要求 AI 符合公平性和透明度法规的受监管行业
信任和声誉依赖于负责任行为的面向公众的 AI 产品
建立 AI 治理框架和伦理审查流程的组织
core_concepts: Fairness metrics: quantifiable measures of equitable treatment across demographic groups and use cases
Model cards: standardized documentation of model capabilities, limitations, and intended use
Accountability chains: clear mapping from every AI decision to a responsible human owner
Red-teaming: adversarial testing to discover bias, safety issues, and misuse vectors before deployment
core_concepts_zh: 公平性指标：跨人群和用例衡量公平对待的可量化度量
模型卡：模型能力、局限性和预期用途的标准化文档
问责链：从每项 AI 决策到负责任人类所有者的清晰映射
红队测试：在部署前发现偏见、安全问题和滥用向量的对抗性测试
timeline: Mitchell et al. publish Model Cards for Model Reporting at FAT* conference
UNESCO adopts the Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
Microsoft publishes its Responsible AI Standard with mandatory internal requirements
EU AI Act formally adopted, establishing legal requirements for AI transparency and fairness
Responsible AI frameworks become standard practice in enterprise AI development worldwide
timeline_zh: Mitchell 等人在 FAT* 会议上发表「模型报告的模型卡」
联合国教科文组织通过人工智能伦理建议书
微软发布负责任 AI 标准，包含强制性内部要求
欧盟 AI 法案正式通过，确立 AI 透明度和公平性的法律要求
负责任 AI 框架成为全球企业 AI 开发的标准实践
dos: Conduct impact assessments early and revisit them as the system evolves
Define measurable fairness metrics and test them across diverse demographic slices
Publish model cards and maintain transparent documentation of system limitations
Establish red-teaming practices and run adversarial exercises before each major release
dos_zh: 尽早开展影响评估并随系统演进重新审视
定义可衡量的公平性指标并在多元人群切片上测试
发布模型卡并维护系统局限性的透明文档
建立红队实践并在每次重大发布前进行对抗演练
donts: Don't treat responsible AI as a checkbox exercise - embed it in the development lifecycle
Don't assume fairness in one context transfers to all contexts - test across diverse scenarios
Don't publish AI systems without clear documentation of known limitations and failure modes
Don't ignore intersectional bias - test fairness across combinations of demographic attributes
donts_zh: 不要将负责任 AI 视为打勾练习——将其嵌入开发生命周期
不要假设一个场景中的公平性可迁移到所有场景——跨多样化场景测试
不要在没有明确记录已知局限和故障模式的情况下发布 AI 系统
不要忽视交叉偏见——跨人口属性组合测试公平性
case_study: Microsoft implemented its Responsible AI Standard across all product teams, requiring impact assessments, fairness testing, and model cards before any AI feature ships. After discovering bias in a facial recognition system, the company established mandatory red-teaming and fairness testing protocols that became an industry reference for responsible AI governance.
case_study_zh: 微软在所有产品团队实施了负责任 AI 标准，要求在任何 AI 功能发布前完成影响评估、公平性测试和模型卡。在发现面部识别系统中的偏见后，公司建立了强制性的红队测试和公平性测试协议，成为行业负责任 AI 治理的参考标准。
when_not_to_use: Internal research experiments that will never be deployed to production
Purely creative AI tools with no decision-making impact on people
Hobby projects with no public users or consequential outputs
Benchmarking or evaluation tasks that don't produce user-facing decisions
when_not_to_use_zh: 永远不会部署到生产环境的内部研究实验
对人没有决策影响的纯创意 AI 工具
没有公共用户或后果性输出的业余项目
不产生面向用户决策的基准测试或评估任务

name: Agent Communication Protocol
desc: Standardize message contracts between autonomous AI agents
desc_zh: 标准化自主 AI 代理之间的消息契约
steps: Define a canonical message envelope: sender, receiver, intent, payload, correlation ID
Adopt or implement a shared registry where agents advertise their capabilities and schemas
Use async message queues for decoupled agent communication to avoid tight coupling
Implement acknowledgment and retry semantics with idempotency keys for reliability
Version all message schemas and enforce backward-compatibility rules as agents evolve
steps_zh: 定义规范消息信封：发送方、接收方、意图、载荷、关联 ID
采用或实现共享注册中心，供代理广播自身能力和消息模式
使用异步消息队列进行松耦合的代理通信，避免强依赖
实现带有幂等键的确认与重试语义以保障可靠性
对所有消息模式进行版本管理，并在代理演进时强制向后兼容
origin_author: FIPA (Foundation for Intelligent Physical Agents); modernized by Agent Protocol (e2b), Anthropic MCP, and Google A2A
origin_source: FIPA Agent Communication Language Specification (2002); Agent Protocol open standard (2023); Google Agent-to-Agent Protocol (2025)
origin_source_zh: FIPA 代理通信语言规范（2002）；Agent Protocol 开放标准（2023）；Google Agent-to-Agent 协议（2025）
when_to_use: Multi-agent systems where agents from different teams or organizations must interoperate
Distributed agent architectures that need reliable, asynchronous message passing
Platforms hosting third-party agents that require standardized communication contracts
Systems where agent capabilities evolve independently and must maintain compatibility
when_to_use_zh: 不同团队或组织的代理需要互操作的多代理系统
需要可靠异步消息传递的分布式代理架构
托管第三方代理、需要标准化通信契约的平台
代理能力独立演进且必须保持兼容性的系统
core_concepts: Message envelope: a standardized wrapper containing sender, receiver, intent, payload, and correlation ID
Capability registry: a shared directory where agents advertise what they can do and what schemas they accept
Async decoupling: using message queues to decouple agents so they can operate independently
Schema versioning: evolving message formats with backward-compatible changes to avoid breaking existing agents
core_concepts_zh: 消息信封：包含发送方、接收方、意图、载荷和关联 ID 的标准化封装
能力注册中心：代理广播自身功能和接受的消息模式的共享目录
异步解耦：使用消息队列解耦代理，使其可独立运行
模式版本控制：以向后兼容的方式演进消息格式，避免破坏现有代理
timeline: FIPA publishes the Agent Communication Language specification for multi-agent systems
e2b launches the Agent Protocol open standard for LLM agent interoperability
Anthropic releases MCP, enabling standardized agent-to-tool communication
Google publishes the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol for cross-platform agent communication
Industry convergence begins around MCP and A2A as complementary agent communication standards
timeline_zh: FIPA 发布面向多代理系统的代理通信语言规范
e2b 推出面向大模型代理互操作的 Agent Protocol 开放标准
Anthropic 发布 MCP，实现标准化的代理到工具通信
Google 发布 Agent-to-Agent（A2A）协议用于跨平台代理通信
行业开始围绕 MCP 和 A2A 作为互补的代理通信标准进行融合
dos: Define a canonical message envelope with clear fields for routing, intent, and correlation
Implement capability discovery so agents can find and understand each other dynamically
Use idempotency keys for all state-changing messages to enable safe retries
Version all message schemas and enforce backward compatibility rules
dos_zh: 定义包含路由、意图和关联字段的规范消息信封
实现能力发现，使代理能动态发现和理解彼此
为所有状态变更消息使用幂等键以实现安全重试
对所有消息模式进行版本管理并强制执行向后兼容规则
donts: Don't let agents communicate via unstructured natural language without schema validation
Don't tightly couple agents with synchronous point-to-point calls in distributed systems
Don't ignore message ordering and delivery guarantees in critical workflows
Don't break backward compatibility in message schemas without a migration path
donts_zh: 不要让代理通过未经模式验证的非结构化自然语言通信
不要在分布式系统中用同步点对点调用紧耦合代理
不要在关键工作流中忽视消息排序和交付保证
不要在没有迁移路径的情况下破坏消息模式的向后兼容性
case_study: Google developed the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol to enable interoperability between AI agents across different platforms and organizations. A2A provides a standardized way for agents to discover each other's capabilities, negotiate communication formats, and exchange structured messages. Combined with MCP for tool access, A2A establishes a comprehensive agent communication stack.
case_study_zh: Google 开发了 Agent-to-Agent（A2A）协议，实现不同平台和组织间 AI 代理的互操作性。A2A 提供了标准化方式让代理发现彼此的能力、协商通信格式并交换结构化消息。结合用于工具访问的 MCP，A2A 建立了完整的代理通信技术栈。
when_not_to_use: Single-agent systems where there is no inter-agent communication needed
Tightly integrated systems where all agents are owned and deployed by the same team
Prototype projects where the overhead of protocol design is premature
Simple tool-calling scenarios where direct function invocation is sufficient
when_not_to_use_zh: 不需要代理间通信的单代理系统
所有代理由同一团队拥有和部署的紧密集成系统
协议设计开销为时过早的原型项目
直接函数调用已足够的简单工具调用场景
